sections.title: Foundational Synthesis — Material Classification & Value Chain Position
required_elements: Material families classification (engineered alloys, advanced ceramics, electronic/optical materials, performance coatings, powder metallurgy)
Value chain layer control vs. dependency assessment
Material specificity and qualification cycle length analysis
Cost-of-failure ratio analysis (material cost % of finished product vs. failure cost multiple)
Raw material input assessment (supply concentration, price volatility, strategic stockpile status)
Leadership materials science expertise and domain continuity
Insider alignment (ownership %, compensation structure, net transaction history)

sections.title: Materials Economics — The Specification-to-Revenue Bridge
required_elements: Key material specifications and customer qualification cycle length by market
Sole-source vs. dual-source position assessment at key customers
Industry specification and regulatory barriers (AMS, ASTM, MIL-SPEC, SEMI standards)
Material cost as % of customer's finished product cost
ASP trajectory by product family (3-5 year trend with above-input-cost pricing)
Pricing mechanism assessment (cost-plus, index-based, or value-based)
Price elasticity by end market (defense/aerospace vs. industrial elasticity)
Raw material input cost decomposition and passthrough mechanisms
Recycling/secondary recovery capabilities and cost/supply hedge benefits
Inventory strategy (strategic stockpiling, carrying costs)
Gross margin decomposition (mix shift, volume leverage, yield, raw material lag, value-add steps)

sections.title: Ecosystem & Competitive Moat Analysis
required_elements: Qualification lock-in architecture (switching cost, re-qualification timeline, regulatory re-approval)
Application engineering assessment (embedded engineers, co-development relationships)
Proprietary IP protection (patents, trade secrets, process technology, coating technology)
Direct competitor identification with scale, cost position, geographic reach comparison
Substitution threat assessment (alternative materials, next-gen designs reducing material)
Vertical integration threat from major customers
Chinese/low-cost competitor entry risk by product family
End-market diversification (aerospace, semiconductor, energy, automotive, medical, general industrial)
End-market cyclicality assessment (secular growth vs. cyclical markets)

sections.title: Financial Logic & Valuation
required_elements: Revenue decomposition by segment with growth rate and gross margin profile
Customer concentration (% from top 5/10 customers)
Cash generation mechanics (revenue → gross margin → R&D → SG&A → operating leverage)
R&D intensity (3-6% of revenue typical for specialty materials)
CapEx intensity (4-8% for maintenance, higher during expansion)
FCF conversion (50-70% of EBITDA typical)
Valuation framework (EV/EBITDA primary 10-14x for quality, P/E, FCF yield, PEG)
ROIC vs. WACC spread and trend
Margin expansion runway assessment
Net cash/debt position and share buyback history

sections.title: Supply Chain & Geopolitical Risk Architecture
required_elements: USGS Critical Minerals List and EU Critical Raw Materials Act exposure
Top 3 producing countries for each critical input
Export restriction risk (China's gallium/germanium/antimony precedent)
Recycling/secondary recovery capability for critical inputs
Customer concentration in specific defense programs, aircraft platforms, semiconductor fabs
Program cancellation/delay/downrate revenue impact assessment
Backlog/order book visibility (% of next 12 months under firm orders)
Tariff exposure (Section 232, 301 coverage)
Friend-shoring/reshoring tailwind assessment
Export control exposure (EAR/ITAR restrictions)

sections.title: Product Roadmap & Innovation Pipeline
required_elements: R&D pipeline (new alloy compositions, advanced ceramics, coatings, additive feedstock)
R&D-to-revenue cycle (3-7 years typical for specialty materials)
Investment in secular growth applications (AI/data center thermal management, EV, space, hypersonics, nuclear)
Manufacturing technology investment (additive, HIP, powder metallurgy, precision rolling)
Capacity expansion projects (timeline, cost, target end markets)
Recycling/closed-loop programs and circular economy positioning
Sustainability/ESG positioning as customer requirement

sections.title: Pattern Matching & IDP Flagging
required_elements: Margin expansion despite flat/declining revenue (mix shift or cost reduction indicator)
Revenue growth concentrated in single end market (secular tailwind or concentration risk?)
Raw material cost increases NOT passed through (pricing power erosion)
R&D spend declining as % of revenue (mature cash cow or underinvestment?)
Customer qualification wins at new OEMs (TAM expansion or backfilling?)
Inventory build relative to revenue trajectory (demand anticipation or channel stuffing?)
Acquisition activity accelerating (platform building or masking organic weakness?)
Insider buying during downturns (conviction signal)
Competitor capacity additions (supply discipline or demand strength?)

sections.title: High-Agency Investigation Tracks
required_elements: Track 1: specific supply chain mapping (mine-to-end-product, government stockpile policy, tech substitution)
Track 2: raw material sourcing and recycling capability assessment
Track 3: customer qualification and program pipeline verification

structural_requirements: All sections present with markdown headings
Material families classified with value chain layer assessment
Qualification cycle length documented by end market (aerospace 12-36 months, semi 6-18 months, auto 6-12 months)
Sole-source vs. dual-source positions quantified at key customers
Material cost as % of finished product calculated (target <5% for specialty moat)
ASP trajectory documented over 3-5 years showing pricing above input cost inflation
Pricing mechanism identified (cost-plus, index-based, or value-based)
Price elasticity assessment by end market
Raw material input costs decomposed by key ingredients (tungsten, cobalt, beryllium, precious metals %)
Passthrough mechanism documented (surcharges, index-linking, fixed-price exposure)
Gross margin decomposed into components (mix shift, volume, yield, lag effects, value-add)
Specification and regulatory barriers documented (industry standards, military specs)
Switching cost quantification (re-qualification time, testing costs, regulatory approvals)
Proprietary IP assessed (patent count, expiry timeline, trade secret processes)
Competitor competitive positioning mapped vs. 2-3 direct competitors
Substitution risks identified (alternative materials, design-outs)
Vertical integration threat assessed (customer scale, economics justification)
Chinese competitor entry risk quantified by product family
End-market revenue decomposition with concentration assessment
End-market cyclicality mapped (secular growth vs. cyclical exposure)
R&D intensity calculated (% of revenue, pipeline assessment)
CapEx intensity documented with expansion project timelines
FCF conversion modeled from revenue through cash generation
Valuation multiple comparison to specialty materials and engineered products peers
ROIC vs. WACC spread calculated and trended
Critical mineral dependencies mapped to USGS/EU lists
Export restriction risk modeled (impact of potential bans by country/material)
Program concentration risk assessed (top 3 programs as % of revenue)
Backlog order book visibility quantified (firm orders % of next 12 months)
R&D pipeline assessed with development timelines to commercialization
Recycling/circular economy capabilities documented
ESG/sustainability positioning as competitive advantage evaluated
Investigation tracks include specific supply chain entities, recycling process capabilities, and customer qualification pipeline
